overview.tagline: An intimate, top-rated school community on the Northern Valley hillside
overview.description: Cresskill is a small, affluent borough nestled in the Northern Valley section of Bergen County. With a population of approximately 9,000, Cresskill is celebrated for its exceptional public schools, safe tree-lined streets, and strong sense of community. The borough has become increasingly diverse, with a notable Korean-American community that has enriched local culture and dining. Its compact size, excellent schools, and proximity to the George Washington Bridge make it highly desirable for families.
highlights: Top-rated public schools — among the best in Bergen County
Close proximity to GWB for NYC commuters
Active recreation programs and community events

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties assessed by the Borough Tax Assessor. Home values are supported by the strong school district.
realEstate.recentTrends: Cresskill's housing market is highly competitive, driven by families seeking entry to the top-rated school district. Homes sell quickly, often above asking price. Teardown-and-rebuild projects are common as buyers invest in the school district.
neighborhoodNotes: The area near Edward H. Bryan School is popular with families seeking walkability to school
Homes along County Road have larger lots and higher price points
The eastern side near Tenafly border features mature tree-lined streets
New construction is replacing older ranch-style homes throughout the borough

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup provided twice weekly.
details: Collection days depend on location within the borough
Sealed bags or covered containers required by 6:00 AM
50 lb weight limit per container
Bulk item pickup available by calling DPW

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Weekly curbside recycling through the Bergen County Utilities Authority.
details: Commingled recyclables in blue bins
Paper and cardboard collected separately
No plastic bags or Styrofoam
Recycling schedule available on borough website

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water provided by Veolia Water. Sewer managed through the borough and BCUA.
details: Quarterly water billing from Veolia
Sewer charges on property tax bills
Report water main issues to DPW
Annual water quality reports available

permits.title: Building Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles all construction and zoning permits.
details: Permits required for all structural, electrical, plumbing, and HVAC work
New construction and teardown-rebuild projects require full site plan review
Zoning Board reviews variance applications
Certificate of Occupancy required

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Residential parking and commuter lot parking managed by the borough.
details: Commuter parking lot available near Union Avenue
Residential street parking generally unrestricted during the day
Overnight parking restrictions on certain streets
Snow emergency parking bans enforced

additionalServices.title: Recreation Programs
additionalServices.description: Active recreation department offering youth and adult programs year-round.
details: Youth sports leagues (soccer, basketball, baseball, lacrosse)
Summer camp and swimming programs
Adult fitness and recreation classes
Community pool open to residents in summer

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Noise regulations protect the quiet residential character of the borough.
keyRules: Construction prohibited before 7:30 AM and after 6:00 PM weekdays
Saturday construction 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM
No construction on Sundays or federal holidays
Leaf blower restrictions apply seasonally

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their lots per borough standards.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained below 10 inches
Snow must be cleared from sidewalks within 24 hours
No junk vehicles or debris accumulation on properties
Fences and structures must be maintained in good repair

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Standard dog licensing and leash requirements enforced.
keyRules: Annual dog license required from Borough Clerk
Dogs must be on leash when off owner's property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately
Rabies vaccination required

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Street parking regulated for safety and neighborhood preservation.
keyRules: Overnight street parking may be restricted on certain roads
No parking during snow emergencies
Commercial vehicles restricted in residential zones
Parking violations subject to ticketing

rentals.title: Rental Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must meet borough inspection standards.
keyRules: Rental registration required
Certificate of Habitability at tenant turnover
Inspections before new tenant occupancy
Landlords responsible for property maintenance compliance

other.title: Tree Removal
other.summary: Tree removal regulated to preserve the borough's leafy character.
keyRules: Permit required for removal of trees above a certain diameter
Replacement trees may be required
Shade Tree Commission oversees tree management
Fines for unauthorized tree removal
other.source: Borough of Cresskill Shade Tree Ordinance

publicSchools.name: Edward H. Bryan School
publicSchools.notes: Primary school with strong academics and parent involvement

publicSchools.name: Merritt Memorial School
publicSchools.notes: Highly rated middle school known for rigorous academics and STEM programs. High school students attend Northern Valley Regional High School at Demarest.

privateSchools.name: St. Therese of Lisieux School
privateSchools.notes: Catholic school in Cresskill serving the parish community

whyMoveHere: Exceptional public schools ranked among Bergen County's finest
Safe, family-oriented community with low crime
Access to Northern Valley Regional High School at Demarest
Close to GWB for a manageable NYC commute

commuteToNYC.primaryMethod: NJ Transit bus to GWB Bus Station
commuteToNYC.notes: NJ Transit buses along County Road provide service to the GWB Bus Station, connecting to the A train. Some residents drive to the GWB (15-20 minutes). Commuter parking available in the borough.

costOfLiving.summary: Cresskill's cost of living is above the Bergen County average, driven by high home prices and property taxes reflecting the premium school district.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Well above the New Jersey average, especially housing costs
costOfLiving.vsNationalAverage: Significantly above the national average across all categories

pros: Very safe community with low crime
Growing cultural diversity enriches dining and community life
cons: Home prices at a premium due to school district demand
Limited commercial amenities within the borough
Teardown-rebuild trend is changing neighborhood character
No train station — bus-dependent for transit to NYC

restaurants.name: Yeolmu
restaurants.description: Contemporary Korean cuisine with a modern twist, popular with locals and visitors.

restaurants.name: Amalfi Ristorante
restaurants.description: Italian fine dining with fresh pasta, seafood, and an elegant atmosphere.

restaurants.name: Manna Korean BBQ
restaurants.description: Authentic Korean barbecue restaurant with tabletop grilling and traditional side dishes.

restaurants.name: Cresskill Bagel & Deli
restaurants.description: Classic NJ bagel shop and deli serving breakfast, sandwiches, and coffee.

parks.name: Brookside Park
parks.description: Community park with athletic fields, playground, basketball courts, and walking paths.

parks.name: Davis Field
parks.description: Large athletic complex with baseball diamonds, soccer fields, and recreation facilities.

parks.name: Veterans Memorial Park
parks.description: Park honoring veterans with memorials, benches, and green space in the center of the borough.

events.name: Cresskill Day
events.description: Annual community celebration with food, games, rides, and live entertainment for all ages.

events.name: Memorial Day Ceremony
events.description: Borough-wide Memorial Day observance honoring fallen service members.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Community gathering with tree lighting, hot chocolate, caroling, and seasonal activities.

attractions.name: Cresskill Public Library
attractions.description: Active community library with programs for all ages, book clubs, and digital resources.

attractions.name: Cresskill Community Pool
attractions.description: Borough pool complex open to residents during summer months with swimming, social events, and swim team programs.

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the second and fourth Monday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Cresskill Borough Hall, 67 Union Avenue, Cresskill, NJ 07626
